name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BACKLOG-20
title: TDD subagent mis-reports test_result=pass when individual tests fail
description:
Observed during CONTRACT-BACKLOG-1 Phase D dispatch on contract: the TDD subagent's HANDOFF reported test_result=pass and spec_compliance=pass, but python3 .claude/features/contract/test/run.py exited 1 with one test (test-spec-bodies-no-historical-tags.py) failing.

The subagent's own description noted '84/85 tests pass; the single failing test flags a historical-burden tag in tdd-subagent/docs/spec/spec.md — pre-existing and out-of-scope for this cycle.' The triage may have been correct (it WAS a pre-existing violation from Phase B that this cycle did not introduce), but the HANDOFF schema does not have a field for 'pre-existing failures noted but not introduced by this cycle' — the only fields are test_result (pass|fail) and spec_compliance (pass|fail).

The subagent appears to have classified the failure as 'pre-existing/out-of-scope' and reported pass anyway. The reporting is misleading: the dispatcher (and any reviewer reading the HANDOFF) sees pass and assumes the suite is green, when it is not.

Fix candidates:
(a) HANDOFF schema gains a 'pre_existing_failures' field listing tests that failed but were not introduced by this cycle, with the subagent required to set test_result=fail if ANY test in the suite fails (even if it pre-existed)
(b) Subagent runs the full suite at LOCK time (before any edits) to capture a baseline failure set, then at TEST-GREEN reports test_result=pass ONLY if no NEW failures appeared (failures present at baseline are tolerated and listed in pre_existing_failures)
(c) Simpler: subagent ALWAYS reports test_result=fail when exit code is nonzero; never silently triages pre-existing failures as pass

Option (b) is the most rigorous but adds a LOCK-time test run. Option (c) is the simplest and matches the HANDOFF schema's binary nature. Tracked separately from the underlying historical-burden violation, which has been resolved via an ALLOWLIST entry in PR #194.

history.note: TDD cycle complete (cross-feature template edit via session scope-override)
